title: Leakage-safe ordered empirical-Bayes stop priors
summary: CatBoost’s ordered target statistics and classic empirical-Bayes smoothing both support the same idea: noisy categorical or binned event rates become stronger when shrunk toward broader parents.
rationale: CatBoost’s ordered target statistics and classic empirical-Bayes smoothing both support the same idea: noisy categorical or binned event rates become stronger when shrunk toward broader parents. This competition has many sparse combinations of Driver, Race, Compound, Stint, TyreLife band, and race phase. A hierarchical stop-rate prior can capture historical strategy tendencies without needing a full simulator.
implementation_hint: Create strictly OOF prior features such as logit pit-rate for (Driver,Compound), (Race,Compound,Stint), (Compound,TyreLifeBin,RacePhase), and nested shrinkage from narrow cells to broader parents. Use leave-race-out or ordered-by-time computation so each row only sees historical counts. Pass counts and posterior means as extra features, not final predictions.
expected_effect: Medium upside on sparse drivers/races and on wet compounds where tree splits alone may underfit rare regimes.
risk: Naive target encoding will leak badly and inflate CV. Overly fine bins can collapse to noise unless strong shrinkage and minimum-count thresholds are enforced.
